title: Refactor error_tuple_handler-adjacent nested case using with + function-head match
prompt:
Given a function that unwraps error tuples by nesting case chains:

```
def pipeline(input) do
  case step1(input) do
    {:ok, v1} ->
      case step2(v1) do
        {:ok, v2} ->
          case step3(v2) do
            {:ok, v3} -> {:ok, v3}
            {:error, r} -> {:error, {:step3, r}}
          end
        {:error, r} -> {:error, {:step2, r}}
      end
    {:error, r} -> {:error, {:step1, r}}
  end
end
```

Rewrite with a `with` expression that uses per-step normalization helpers `do_step1/1`, `do_step2/1`, `do_step3/1` — each wraps `step<n>/1` and tags errors. The top-level `with` has no `else`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Pipeline do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Three-step helper extraction + per-step error tagging. Synthesis of multiple capabilities.
